1377	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
1378	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
1379	
1380	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
1381	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
1382	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
1383	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
1384	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
1385	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
1386	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
1387	
1388	\begin{theorem}
1389	
1390	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
1391	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
1392	  
1393	  % NOTE (iter-179 review, lvb-checker `codimone-iter179` corrective):
1394	  % `\lean{...}` pin DETACHED (Path D2 option (b) per the lvb-checker). The
1395	  % substantive Hartshorne-II.6 valuative reformulation (the iff with
1396	  % ord_W >= 0) below is the *intended* statement; the iter-179 Lane D
1397	  % `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` decl is only a 2-LOC
1398	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` reshuffle that does NOT prove the
1399	  % ord_W content, so re-pinning `\lean{...}` to it would falsely claim
1400	  % kernel-clean formalization of the substantive Hartshorne iff. The
1401	  % weaker reshuffle proven this iter will be pinned to its own light
1402	  % `\begin{lemma}` block (working title `lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle`)
1403	  % by an iter-180+ blueprint-writer dispatch; the substantive Hartshorne
1404	  % version stays pinned here but unlinked until a future
1405	  % `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg` lemma carries
1406	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.order` machinery (pullback `RationalMap →
1407	  % function-field ring map`, not yet in Mathlib at b80f227). See
1408	  % task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-codimone-iter179.md +
1409	  % proof-journal/sessions/session_179/recommendations.md REC-14.
1410	  % NOTE (iter-194 writer): no corresponding Lean declaration found in
1411	  % CodimOneExtension.lean --- iter-195 must either rename to match an
1412	  % existing decl or add a new Lean stub. (The iter-179 detach was
1413	  % structural, not a \lean{...} typo: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6
1414	  % ord_W >= 0 reformulation requires the
1415	  % Scheme.RationalMap-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
1416	  % not in Mathlib at commit b80f227 and is not yet built in this
1417	  % project. The weaker iter-179 reshuffle Lean declaration
1418	  % mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point is blueprint-pinned
1419	  % at \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} as the honest
1420	  % fallback; thm:weil_divisor_obstruction here remains unpinned at the
1421	  % \lean{...} level until the genuine pullback decl lands.)
1422	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation \(v_Y\) associated to a
1423	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}\) as the
1424	  % subring of \(K(X)\) on which \(v_Y \geq 0\)) (read from
1425	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
1426	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
1427	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
1428	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
1429	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
1430	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).
1431	  % Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has a \emph{zero} along
1432	  % \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\) has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of
1433	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
1434	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
1435	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
1436	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
1437	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
1438	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
1439	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
1440	  \begin{enumerate}
1441	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
1442	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
1443	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
1444	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
1445	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
1446	      satisfying
1447	      \[
1448	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
1449	      \]
1450	  \end{enumerate}
1451	
1452	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
1453	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
1454	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
1455	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
1456	\end{theorem}
1457	
1458	\begin{proof}
1459	  
1460	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
1461	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
1462	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
1463	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
1464	  \[
1465	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
1466	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
1467	  \]
1468	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
1469	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
1470	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
1471	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
1472	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
1473	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
1474	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
1475	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
1476	
1477	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
1478	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
1479	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
1480	\end{proof}
1481	
1482	\begin{remark}
1483	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
1484	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
1485	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
1486	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
1487	  isolation, the abstract codim-\(\geq 2\) statement
1488	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
1489	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
1490	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
1491	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-\(0\) classifying-map
1492	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
1493	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
1494	\end{remark}
1495	
1496	\begin{lemma}
1497	
1498	\leanok
1499	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
1500	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
1501	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
1502	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
1503	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
1504	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
1505	  following are equivalent:
1506	  \begin{enumerate}
1507	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
1508	      \(W\)).
1509	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
1510	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
1511	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
1512	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
1513	  \end{enumerate}
1514	\end{lemma}
1515	
1516	\begin{proof}
1517	
1518	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
1519	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
1520	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
1521	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
1522	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
1523	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
1524	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
1525	  the unfolded definition.
1526	\end{proof}
1527	
1528	\begin{remark}
1529	  \label{rmk:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle_scope}
1530	  \textbf{Relationship to \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}.}
1531	  \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} is the iter-179 Lane~D honest
1532	  fallback for the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 ``regular = no pole''
1533	  reformulation that \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} states in its full
1534	  \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form. The reshuffle captures only the
1535	  definitional half of the equivalence (existence of a partial-map
1536	  representative through a prime-divisor generic point); upgrading it to the
1537	  substantive \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form requires the
1538	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
1539	  not shipped by Mathlib at commit \texttt{b80f227}. See the iter-179
1540	  \texttt{\% NOTE} block on \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} for the
1541	  history.
1542	\end{remark}

1656	\section{Out of scope}
1657	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
1658	
1659	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
1660	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
1661	scope:
1662	\begin{itemize}
1663	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular \(\Rightarrow\)
1664	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
1665	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
1666	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
1667	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
1668	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
1669	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
1670	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
1671	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
1672	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
1673	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
1674	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
1675	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
1676	    \(\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A\) / unirational\(\dashrightarrow A\) constancy
1677	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
1678	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-\(0\) base case is
1679	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut
1680	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
1681	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
1682	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve.} The proof of
1683	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
1684	    smooth surface \(X \times X\) but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
1685	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-\(1\)
1686	    decomposition \(\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty\) on a nonsingular surface is
1687	    used. The bilinear degree formula on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve (intersection
1688	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
1689	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
1690	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
1691	    introduced.
1692	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
1693	    project's positive-genus arm runs over \(\bar k\) throughout; descent of the
1694	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
1695	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
1696	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
1697	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
1698	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
1699	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for \(G\) a group variety is sharper than any
1700	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original \(X\)
1701	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
1702	\end{itemize}

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38	
39	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
40	
41	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
42	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
43	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
44	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
45	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
46	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
47	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
48	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
49	
50	The 6 pinned declarations are:
51	
52	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
53	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
54	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
55	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
56	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
57	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
58	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
59	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
60	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
61	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
62	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
63	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
64	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
65	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
66	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
67	   header).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107	
108	## References
109

111	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
113	-/

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194

1356	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1357	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1358	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1359	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:

1383	
1384	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1385	
1386	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1387	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1388	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1389	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1390	
1391	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1392	
1393	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1394	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1395	
1396	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1397	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1398	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1399	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1400	
1401	The proof combines two steps:
1402	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1403	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1404	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1405	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1406	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1407	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1408	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1409	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1410	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1411	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1412	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1413	
1414	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1415	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1416	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1417	
1418	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1419	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1420	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1421	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1422	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1423	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1424	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1425	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1426	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1427	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1428	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1429	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1430	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1431	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1432	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1433	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1434	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1435	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1436	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1437	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1438	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1439	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:

1461	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1462	
1463	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1464	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1465	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1466	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.

1470	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1471	codim 1.**
1472	
1473	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1474	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1475	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1476	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1477	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1478	
1479	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1480	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1481	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1482	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1483	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1484	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1485	
1486	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1487	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/

1492	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1493	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1494	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}

1496	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1497	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1498	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1499	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1500	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1501	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by

1503	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1504	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4

692	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
693	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
694	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
695	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`

698	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
699	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
700	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
701	  induction n with
702	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
703	  | succ n ih =>
704	    intro m _
705	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
706	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
707	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
708	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
709	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
710	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
711	    have ih' := ih p
712	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
713	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
714	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
715	    gcongr

817	/-! ### Iter-201 Step A2 substrate (cotangent linear-independence transport)
818

822	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` linear-independence statement

824	
825	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` — the
826	  `S`-lin-indep version of the family `i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, _⟩`,

828	  `basisCotangent_apply`.
829	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized`
830	  — the same family transported through the canonical localisation map
831	  `Cot →ₗ[S] LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Cot` at any prime ideal `p` of `S`,
832	  yielding `Localization.AtPrime p`-lin-indep. This composes the previous
833	  helper with Mathlib's `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule`.
834	
835	Together these realise Analogue D of the analogist recipe (lin-indep

837	chain — identifying `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`

843	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive
844	presentation `P : R → S` of an `R`-algebra, the family
845	`i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩`
846	in `P.toExtension.Cotangent` is `S`-linearly independent.
847	
848	This is a forward-ergonomics re-package of `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent`
849	+ `P.basisCotangent_apply`: the basis-form lin-indep statement is restated
850	with the explicit `Cotangent.mk`-of-relation form on the consumer side, so
851	downstream callers do not need to manually unfold the basis abstraction.
852	
853	Axiom-clean: 1 `Basis.linearIndependent` + 1 funext rewrite. -/
854	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent
855	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
856	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
857	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx) :
858	    LinearIndependent S
859	      (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
860	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) := by
861	  have h := P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent
862	  have heq : (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
863	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) = P.basisCotangent := by
864	    funext i
865	    exact (P.basisCotangent_apply i).symm
866	  rw [heq]
867	  exact h
868	
869	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 2.** Transport of
870	`submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` through the
871	canonical localisation map of `P.toExtension.Cotangent` at any prime `p` of
872	`S`. The resulting family in `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
873	is `Localization.AtPrime p`-linearly independent.
874	
875	Composes the previous helper with Mathlib's
876	`LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` (in
877	`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) and the canonical
878	`LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap` + `LocalizedModule.isModule`-driven
879	`IsLocalizedModule` instance on the generic `LocalizedModule` construction.

881	This is Analogue D of the `coe-stacks00sw` mathlib-analogist recipe at the
882	level of the un-quotiented cotangent module; the residual A2 work
883	(identifying the localised cotangent with `(I·A)/(I·A)²` over `A = S_p`,
884	i.e.\ Stacks 02JK's conormal-localisation iso) remains a Mathlib gap and is
885	not landed here.

888	of `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent`. -/
889	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized
890	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
891	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
892	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx)
893	    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :
894	    LinearIndependent (Localization.AtPrime p)
895	      (fun i : sx =>
896	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
897	          (Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
898	            (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker))) :=
899	  LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl
900	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
901	    (submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent P)
902

906	obligation, currently a Mathlib gap), the Krull dimension of the

912	Krull dimension) with the iter-200 additive form

922	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_localization_MvPolynomial_of_regular.{v}
923	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ix : Type v} [Finite ix]
924	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ix k)) [m.IsMaximal]
925	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ix k) A]
926	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] [IsNoetherianRing A]
927	    (rs : List A) (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A rs)
928	    {b : ℕ} (hLen : rs.length = b) :
929	    ringKrullDim (A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) =
930	      (Nat.card ix : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
931	  haveI : IsLocalRing A := IsLocalization.AtPrime.isLocalRing A m
932	  exact ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence rs hReg
933	    (ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial m A) hLen

976	  whose carrier is a subsingleton (e.g.\ `Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`), every
977	  nonempty open is `⊤`; hence the sections of `Spec (.of k̄)` over any nonempty

994	private theorem exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
995	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
996	    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
997	    ∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
998	      ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n :=
999	  h.out
